steps.title: Use the setting effect
steps.content: The passage says lab studies allow researchers to control variables, but white-throated sparrows' surroundings can significantly affect their hormone levels. Therefore, a predictable finding would involve a hormone difference between captive sparrows and wild sparrows.

steps.title: Eliminate irrelevant findings
steps.content: Choice A compares wild male sparrows with captive male degus, but the setup concerns white-throated sparrows and how their surroundings affect hormones. Choice C describes differences between individuals within captive and wild groups, not a difference caused by captive versus wild settings.

steps.title: Choose the setting difference
steps.content: Choice B says captive and wild sparrows usually have very similar hormone levels, which conflicts with the claim that surroundings significantly affect hormone levels. Choice D gives a concrete captive-versus-wild hormone difference: captive sparrows have higher baseline corticosterone than wild sparrows. The answer is D.
